name: Pocket Bread
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 0:45
Combine yeast, sugar, and water; let stand for 5 minutes. Make a well in the center of flour in a large mixing bowl; add salt, oil, and half of the yeast mixture. Stir thoroughly. Add remaining yeast mixture, beating well. Turn dough out onto a well-floured surface. Divide dough into two halves; cover one half, and set aside. Divide one portion of dough into 10 equal pieces; knead each piece for 2 minutes or until smooth and elastic. Shape each piece into a ball, and place seam side down on a towel. Cover with another towel; place a damp towel on top. Repeat process with the remaining half of dough. Let dough rise in a warm place (85 degrees), free from drafts, for 1 to 1-1/2 hours or until slightly puffy.
Place each ball of dough onto a well-floured surface; roll into a 6-inch circle. Shake off excess flour, and place on dry towels; cover again with dry towels, then with damp towels. Place plastic wrap on top. Let dough rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 1 hour or until slightly puffy. Lift circles carefully, and place 1/2 inch apart on lightly greased baking sheets; do not stretch dough. Place oven rack 2 inches from the bottom heating element. Bake at 475 degrees for 4 to 6 minutes or until the bottom is lightly browned (it will puff, forming a pocket). Immediately turn oven to broil. Broil 4 inches from the broiler element for 30 to 60 seconds or until lightly browned (watch carefully to prevent overbrowning). Place on towels. When cool to touch, flatten each piece by gently pressing down with fingertips. Repeat baking process with remaining bread.
Store bread in airtight containers. Yield: 20 pocket rounds.
NOTE: Bread may be frozen. Thaw and reheat at 300 degrees for 5 minutes.
